# Six Kingdoms

> Ecological design practice based on South Africa's Garden Route. We build natural swimming pools (EcoPools), restore fynbos, clear invasive alien plants, and help landowners understand and work with their land rather than against it.

## About

Six Kingdoms has been working on the Garden Route for over 10 years. Founded by Matthew Koehorst, the practice works with the specific soils, water flows, and ecology of each site rather than applying generic templates. Every project starts with the land itself.

**Location:** Garden Route, Western Cape, South Africa
**Service areas:** Knysna, Plettenberg Bay, George, Wilderness, Sedgefield, Mossel Bay
**Contact:** matthew@sixkingdoms.org | +27 (0)76 618 9722
**Website:** https://sixkingdoms.org
**Instagram:** https://www.instagram.com/six_kingdoms/

## Services

### EcoPools (Natural Swimming Pools)
Chemical-free swimming pools filtered entirely by biology — aquatic plants and beneficial micro-organisms replace chlorine. The water is clean, soft on skin, and supports biodiversity. EcoPools are built in partnership with EcoPools Africa, South Africa's leading natural pool technology provider.
- No chlorine or harsh chemicals
- Lower long-term running costs than conventional pools
- Supports frogs, dragonflies, and beneficial invertebrates
- Designed for the specific conditions of the Garden Route climate
- Page: https://sixkingdoms.org/ecopools.html

### Ecological Design
Fynbos gardens, food forests, rain gardens, and indigenous landscapes designed to thrive without constant intervention. We work with what is already on a site and help it become more of itself.
- Fynbos gardens and habitat restoration
- Food forests and edible landscapes
- Rain gardens and water management
- Biodiversity corridors connecting habitat patches
- Workshops and education for landowners
- Page: https://sixkingdoms.org/projects.html

### Land Management & Invasive Plant Control
Invasive alien plant clearing, indigenous vegetation restoration, and long-term land stewardship. We help landowners achieve ecological compliance, improve biodiversity, and build resilient landscapes.
- Invasive alien plant identification and removal
- Indigenous replanting and habitat restoration
- Holistic land management planning
- Working for Water programme compliance support
- Long-term stewardship and monitoring
- Page: https://sixkingdoms.org/land-management.html

## Key Facts

- Operating on the Garden Route since 2014 (10+ years)
- 10+ EcoPools built across Knysna, Plettenberg Bay, George, and Wilderness
- 5,000 m² of indigenous gardens planted
- 30+ hectares of land restored
- Official EcoPools Africa partnership (certified natural pool technology)
- Serves both residential and commercial clients

## Project Planning Tool

An interactive web tool for planning EcoPools, ecological gardens, and land management projects on the Garden Route. Includes a cost calculator, indigenous plant finder, land checklist, and vegetable bed planner.
- Page: https://sixkingdoms.org/plan.html

## Frequently Asked Questions

**What is an EcoPool?**
An EcoPool is a natural swimming pool filtered by plants and beneficial micro-organisms instead of chemicals like chlorine. The water is clean, clear, and soft on skin. They are also called natural swimming pools or bio-pools.

**How are EcoPools different from conventional swimming pools?**
EcoPools use a planted regeneration zone — aquatic plants and beneficial bacteria do the filtering work instead of chemicals. Over time they require less maintenance than a conventional pool, produce no chemical waste, and actively support local biodiversity.

**What areas does Six Kingdoms service?**
Six Kingdoms primarily services Knysna, Plettenberg Bay, George, Wilderness, Sedgefield, and surrounding communities on South Africa's Garden Route.

**What does ecological design involve?**
Ecological design means working with the natural patterns of a site — its soils, water flows, sun angles, and existing biodiversity — to create landscapes that thrive without constant intervention. We use indigenous plants, restore degraded areas, and build landscapes that become more resilient over time.

**What is invasive alien plant clearing?**
Invasive alien plants (IAPs) are non-native species that spread aggressively and crowd out indigenous vegetation. Clearing them is required by South African law under the National Environmental Management: Biodiversity Act. Six Kingdoms provides identification, removal, and ongoing management services.

**Do you offer ongoing maintenance?**
Yes. Six Kingdoms provides ongoing land management and stewardship services, with regular consultations, on-site support, and training to help landowners care for their land independently.

**How do I get started?**
Contact matthew@sixkingdoms.org or use the contact form at https://sixkingdoms.org/contact.html. We arrange an initial conversation to understand your site and goals, then outline the best path forward.
